threats.title: Injective Labs GitHub Compromise Pushes Wallet-Key-Stealing npm Packages
threats.summary: Unknown threat actors compromised the Injective Labs SDK project's GitHub repository and leveraged it to publish a malicious package on the npm registry to steal cryptocurrency wallet private keys and mnemonic seed phrases. threats.title: Six New U-Boot Flaws Could Let Malicious Images Crash Devices or Run Code at Boot
threats.summary: Researchers at firmware security firm Binarly have found six new flaws in U-Boot, the small program that starts up hardware as varied as home routers, smart cameras, and the management chips inside data-center servers. Four of the bugs can crash a device. The other two could let an attacker who slips a malicious image in front of the bootloader run their own code, before the device

threats.title: Laser Attack Resets Tangem Wallet Passwords on Cards That Can't Be Patched
threats.summary: Researchers at Ledger's Donjon security team have shown that a precisely timed laser pulse, aimed at the chip inside a Tangem crypto wallet card, can reset the card's password to anything the attacker picks. No old password. No backup card. Once it is reset, whoever did it controls the wallet and can move the coins out. This is not an emergency for most owners. The attack needs

threats.title: Researcher Details WhatsApp-to-Host Attack Chain Using Three OpenClaw Flaws
threats.summary: Details have emerged about three now-patched security flaws in the OpenClaw personal artificial intelligence (AI) assistant that, if successfully exploited, could enable credential theft, privilege escalation, and arbitrary code execution on the host.
